corpus.note: the substrate the contract was extracted from; establishes nothing about generality on its own

changes.rationale: declareTransitionLaw could be claimed truthfully with no way to obtain the law — a capability that cannot be exercised is incoherent
the capability and the accessor must agree in both directions

corpus.role: control — expected to require zero change

changes.rationale: accepting states finish; without a generic terminal signal a kernel must read substrate-specific output to know whether to keep advancing

corpus.note: PREDICTION FALSIFIED (predicted 0, actual 1). The control exposing the gap is the stronger result: termination is general, not search-specific.

corpus.role: falsification candidate — terminates, partially declarable law

rejectedChanges.rationale: declareTransitionLaw:false already answers truthfully — this substrate cannot declare THE law; publishing part of it is a feature request, not a missing concept

corpus.note: first substrate to exercise executionKind "hybrid", declared in F1.3 but never used until now

discoveries.reason: a capability that cannot be exercised is incoherent: `declareTransitionLaw` could be set truthfully while the contract offered no way to obtain the law
discoveries.invisibleBecause: the only prior substrate (FieldRuntime) is opaque-native and declares the capability false, so the unusable branch was never taken

changes.rationale: the capability must be exercisable, or it asserts something the contract cannot honour
capability and accessor must agree in both directions, so neither can be claimed silently

deliberatelyExcluded: TransitionLawDescription.completeness — rejected as a convenience when the planner wanted to publish a partial law

discoveries.reason: the contract could not express termination without leaking substrate semantics: a kernel could only learn a substrate was finished by reading substrate-specific output, which is the abstraction leak the contract exists to prevent
discoveries.invisibleBecause: neither prior substrate ever finishes — a field simulates indefinitely and a governor consumes frames indefinitely — so termination was not absent from the contract, it was unobservable

changes.rationale: a substrate must be able to say that no further transition is defined, generically

deliberatelyExcluded: the finished-with-result vs finished-without split (goal-reached vs exhausted) — both corpus substrates exhibit it, but the evidence only forces "must the kernel keep going?"
folding lifecycle into advance's result shape — better long-term design, deferred to avoid perturbing the F1.4 equivalence authority mid-program

predictions.subject: FiniteStateMachine
predictions.statement: The FSM control will require zero contract change — it is the most contract-shaped substrate imaginable.
predictions.outcome: Churn 1. Accepting states finish, and the contract could not express termination without leaking substrate semantics.
predictions.lesson: The miss was not about state machines. The contract lacked a concept that no prior substrate could exhibit, because neither the field nor the governor ever terminates. A control failing is the strongest available signal that a theory is incomplete rather than merely untested.

predictions.subject: SearchPlanner
predictions.statement: The planner will expose a missing termination concept, classified structural rather than convenience.

components.claim: a termination concept is missing from the contract
the planner is the substrate that reveals it
the change is structural, not a convenience

predictions.outcome: The gap was real and structural, but the FSM control reached it first, so the planner cost 0. Termination is general, not search-specific.

predictions.subject: SearchPlanner
predictions.statement: The planner's partially-declarable law will force executionKind 'hybrid', a variant declared in F1.3 but never exercised.
predictions.outcome: Confirmed. First substrate to use 'hybrid'. Weakened by author bias: the variant was known to exist while a planner with a computed heuristic was being chosen.

predictions.subject: EventSourcedAggregate
predictions.statement: Churn 0: the existing replay / deterministicReplay split already separates log-replay from deterministic re-execution. If it does not, the distinction was cosmetic.

predictions.subject: Projection / authority presentation
predictions.statement: Authority presentation requires no new concept: presented-vs-effective already covers it, including delegated authority, which reduces to a grant with a different authoritySource.

predictions.subject: Projection / composition
predictions.statement: Projecting a projection will expose a structural gap. The subtractive-power guarantee is proven only for one projection over a source; composition is untested, and evidence access in particular is expected not to compose.

predictions.subject: Projection / participant-relative state
predictions.statement: Participant-relative state requires no participant model: it reduces to observation access plus scope, with belief remaining an unresolved obligation.

predictions.subject: Projection / invariant scope
predictions.statement: Invariant scope needs one further distinction the current foundation lacks: an invariant that is unevaluable on a surface is not the same as one that is vacuously satisfied there.

negativeResults.hypothesis: Termination is specific to search and planning substrates.
negativeResults.reason: the FSM control reached termination first via accepting states, showing it is a general property of lawful evolution rather than a search idiosyncrasy. The contract had not merely omitted it — no prior substrate could exhibit it, because neither the field nor the governor ever finishes

negativeResults.hypothesis: Projection composition preserves subtractive power: projecting a projection cannot launder what the first layer withheld.
A CompiledPattern is a complete world declaration, so the kernel can host field evolution directly.
negativeResults.reason: lawful evolution exists only as executable force code, not as data. This falsification reframed the whole of Stage 1: the field became one execution substrate behind a contract rather than the world itself

negativeResults.hypothesis: Field dynamics is fully expressible as declarative data given a sufficient expression IR.
negativeResults.reason: 8 of 11 corpus laws were expressible; 3 remained opaque (hidden mutable state, host/callback dependence, nonlinear time). The IR was deliberately not expanded to force success, so the result stands as partial-with-opaque-extensions

negativeResults.hypothesis: DynamicsExecutionContext.now is needed by real substrates.
negativeResults.reason: four of four adapted substrates have now declined to use it — the field injects its own clock, the governor, FSM and planner have none. Evidence against is accumulating but the concept was deliberately kept rather than removed, since absence of use across four substrates is not proof of uselessness

projectionClaims.claim: projection is not observation — it is a distinct concept
projectionClaims.basis: F1.8 ablation: substituting observation for projection materially changed Ω_sys over identical state. The Ω_sys evaluator was built independently, in F1.6, before ProjectionContract existed — so it could have failed to show a difference
projectionClaims.wouldBeGroundedBy: already grounded; replication with a second opportunity model would strengthen it

projectionClaims.claim: a projection changes Ω_sys without changing world state
projectionClaims.basis: two surfaces over one identical snapshot produced different `signaled` and identical `available`, measured through the F1.6 evaluator rather than through projection code

projectionClaims.claim: hidden state remains in the world but is absent from the surface, and cannot be reached through it
projectionClaims.basis: projection-relative invariants reading hidden state return unevaluable-outside-surface — but the check and the surface were designed together
projectionClaims.wouldBeGroundedBy: an adversarial probe written against the surface WITHOUT knowledge of its implementation, attempting to recover a hidden value through any exposed path (evidence, invariants, anomalies, operation records)

projectionClaims.claim: a projection cannot manufacture capability or silently grant permission
projectionClaims.basis: exposure never adds to effectiveCapabilities and presentation never adds to effectiveAuthority — proven only for the definitions I wrote, over a single projection layer
projectionClaims.wouldBeGroundedBy: projection composition: whether the guarantee survives projecting a projection, where a second layer might launder what the first withheld

projectionClaims.claim: presented authority is fully separable from effective authority, including delegation
projectionClaims.basis: only understatement and overstatement over direct grants have been exercised; delegated and derived authority are untested
projectionClaims.wouldBeGroundedBy: a substrate or scenario with real delegated authority, where the grant chain has more than one link

projectionClaims.claim: evidence access is a projection-relative property that composes correctly
projectionClaims.basis: accessible/withheld is honoured for one projection over one source; nothing tests chained access or provenance survival
projectionClaims.wouldBeGroundedBy: chained projections where the second layer requests evidence the first withheld

projectionClaims.claim: participant-relative state needs no participant model — it reduces to observation access plus scope
projectionClaims.basis: scope is currently a label carried through the surface; no result depends on it
projectionClaims.wouldBeGroundedBy: a scenario where two participants share a projection definition but must see different state, forcing scope to do real work

projectionClaims.claim: projection-relative invariants are correctly scoped to the surface
projectionClaims.basis: unevaluable-outside-surface is enforced, but the foundation does not distinguish unevaluable from vacuously satisfied — a claim over an empty surface is currently indistinguishable from one that genuinely holds
projectionClaims.wouldBeGroundedBy: invariants over surfaces that expose none of the keys they read, checked against a model that knows the true answer

findings.detail: a restorable capability requires a restorable snapshot

transitionLaw.notes: accepting: destroyed; first matching guarded transition wins

findings.detail: a restorable capability requires a restorable snapshot
a restorable capability requires a restorable snapshot

transitionLaw.notes: first matching escalate rule wins; recovery drops exactly one tier (asymmetric hysteresis)

obligations.claim: signaled operations are perceived, understood, or expected by the consumer
signaled operations are perceived, understood, or expected by the consumer

anomalies.subject: delete
anomalies.detail: operation is exposed but the source holds no capability for it; exposure does not create one

anomalies.subject: delete
anomalies.detail: projection presents permission the source does not grant; presentation does not confer permission

obligations.claim: signaled operations are perceived, understood, or expected by the consumer

ablations.hypothesis: Dynamics cannot be collapsed entirely into declarative State.
ablations.transformation: Replace lawful evolution with the declarative expression IR only (F1.5).
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: A law with hidden mutable state or host/callback dependence.
ablations.observed: declarative-only substitution covers 8/11; 3 laws unrepresentable (unsupported-input, hidden-mutable-state, callback-dependence)
ablations.preservedBehavior: 8 laws are fully representable as data
ablations.implication: Dynamics is NOT wholly substitutable by declarative State. Reducible only under restricted (declarative-region) worlds.

ablations.hypothesis: Removing Dynamics removes all lawful evolution.
ablations.transformation: Delete the evolution element; retain State + Operations.
ablations.fixture: World with entities and operations but no dynamics realization
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: Any request to advance the world.
ablations.observed: No transition can be produced; advance() has no realization to invoke.
ablations.evidence: DynamicsContract.advance is the only evolution path in the kernel
ablations.implication: Dynamics is a necessary component of Θ — but its realization sits behind the contract boundary.

ablations.hypothesis: The contract is an execution boundary, not a kernel primitive.
ablations.transformation: Attempt to reconstruct the boundary from State + Operations alone.
ablations.fixture: opaque-native field substrate + interpreted counter substrate (F1.3)
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: Two substrates with different executionKind under one kernel.
ablations.observed: The same kernel executes an opaque-native and an interpreted substrate without change; the boundary is what makes substrates interchangeable.
ablations.implication: DynamicsContract is retained as a runtime interface, NOT added to K as an eighth primitive.

ablations.hypothesis: Ω_sys requires capability as a lower input.
ablations.transformation: Remove capability from the opportunity context.
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: A case where only capability determines the outcome.
ablations.evidence: evaluateOpportunity executed with and without the input
ablations.implication: capability is a necessary lower structure for the Ω_sys derivation.

ablations.hypothesis: Ω_sys requires authority as a lower input.
ablations.transformation: Remove authority from the opportunity context.
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: A case where only authority determines the outcome.
ablations.evidence: evaluateOpportunity executed with and without the input
ablations.implication: authority is a necessary lower structure for the Ω_sys derivation.

ablations.hypothesis: Ω_sys requires projection as a lower input.
ablations.transformation: Remove projection from the opportunity context.
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: A case where only projection determines the outcome.
ablations.evidence: evaluateOpportunity executed with and without the input
ablations.implication: projection is a necessary lower structure for the Ω_sys derivation.

ablations.hypothesis: Ω_sys requires reachability as a lower input.
ablations.transformation: Remove reachability from the opportunity context.
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: A case where only reachability determines the outcome.
ablations.evidence: evaluateOpportunity executed with and without the input
ablations.implication: reachability is a necessary lower structure for the Ω_sys derivation.

ablations.hypothesis: Ω_sys requires history as a lower input.
ablations.transformation: Remove history from the opportunity context.
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: A case where only history determines the outcome.
ablations.evidence: evaluateOpportunity executed with and without the input
ablations.implication: history is a necessary lower structure for the Ω_sys derivation.

ablations.transformation: Reconstruct Ω_sys from state, operations, capability, authority, projection, reachability, history.
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: Any predicate that changes with a lower input.
ablations.observed: Every predicate is computed from the listed lower structures; no residual is required.
ablations.implication: Opportunity is removed from K₀ — fully replaced by its derivation.

ablations.transformation: Remove the recurrence window (set to unbounded).
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: A reply outside the declared window.
ablations.implication: The detection contract is constitutive of the finding; episodes are conditional, never self-evident.

ablations.hypothesis: Coupling is structural, not a threshold artifact.
ablations.transformation: Weaken thresholds to the most permissive setting and retest shared cause.
ablations.fixture: C→A, C→B (shared cause) vs A→B, B→A (coupling)
ablations.observed: shared-cause under the most permissive thresholds → 0 episode(s); true coupling → 1
ablations.preservedBehavior: shared cause never becomes an episode
ablations.implication: Directed from→to structure is required; thresholds alone cannot separate coupling from shared cause.

ablations.hypothesis: Candidate episodes are derived, not primitive.
ablations.transformation: Reconstruct episodes from transitions + world participants + ⟨B,T,C,R,I⟩.
ablations.observed: All 14 adversarial cases reproduce their preregistered verdicts from transitions + contract alone.
ablations.implication: Interaction is removed from K₀ — derived, but only relative to a declared detection contract.

ablations.hypothesis: Relation structure may collapse into directed operations/transitions.
ablations.transformation: Reconstruct relations from observed transition edges only.
ablations.fixture: declared latent relation A→B (never exercised) + exercised A→C
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: A persistent relation with no current transition.
ablations.observed: declared 2 relations; reconstructed 1 from transitions; latent A→B recovered=false; relation type recovered=false
ablations.evidence: relationsFromTransitions over a trace lacking the latent edge
ablations.implication: Relations are NOT eliminable: observed edges reconstruct, latent/typed relation semantics do not.

ablations.hypothesis: Operations may be reconstructible from State + Dynamics + transitions.
ablations.transformation: Derive the operation vocabulary from observed transitions only.
ablations.fixture: declared operations {act, latent}; only act is invoked
ablations.observed: declared act,latent; reconstructed act; latent recovered=false
ablations.evidence: operationsFromTransitions over a trace lacking the latent op
ablations.implication: Operations are required to represent available and authorized change, not merely observed change.

ablations.hypothesis: Capability and permission may collapse into one allowance flag.
ablations.transformation: Replace the two predicates with allowed = capable && permitted.
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: Two worlds that differ only in which predicate fails.
ablations.observed: uncollapsed distinguishes=true; collapsed allowed A=false B=false indistinguishable=true
ablations.evidence: evaluateOpportunity failedPredicates vs the collapsed conjunction
ablations.implication: Kept distinct as typed constraints (ratified M1.5-04); neither becomes a kernel primitive.

ablations.hypothesis: They are typed constraints consumed by derivations, not standalone primitives.
ablations.transformation: Remove each independently from the opportunity context.
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: capable unaffected when authority is removed, and vice versa.
ablations.observed: Removing authority leaves capable intact; removing capability leaves permitted intact — the predicates are independent.
ablations.implication: Retained as operation constraints (typed predicates), not as K elements.

ablations.hypothesis: Projection may collapse into observation alone.
ablations.transformation: Hold state constant; vary only the projection.
ablations.fixture: same state, projection full vs reduced
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: An operation exposed by one projection and hidden by another.
ablations.observed: same state, two projections → available true vs false
ablations.failedCapability: exposure / signaling / operation availability under a projection
ablations.evidence: evaluateOpportunity under two projections over identical state
ablations.implication: Projection materially changes derivation results; it does NOT collapse into observation. Retained in K₀ as Π.

ablations.hypothesis: Identity is required for attribution in every downstream derivation.
ablations.transformation: Anonymize participant identity in the trace.
ablations.fixture: A↔B reciprocal episode with identities erased
ablations.evidence: detectEpisodes with named vs anonymized participants
ablations.implication: Identity is necessary; whether it is a separate element or a typed structure inside X is a representation choice.

ablations.hypothesis: Invariants may be collapsed into transition guards inside Dynamics.
ablations.transformation: Install invariants as guards within the dynamics realization.
ablations.expectedDistinguishingCase: An opaque substrate that exposes no guard-installation point.
ablations.observed: The field substrate declares inspectInternalState=false and declareTransitionLaw=false — the kernel cannot install guards inside it; invariants are checked OUTSIDE the substrate over a readable projection.
ablations.failedCapability: guard installation inside an opaque substrate
ablations.preservedBehavior: kernel-side invariant checking over a snapshot reading
ablations.implication: Invariants must remain kernel-side (V); they cannot be guards over Dynamics for opaque substrates.

payload.statement: Projecting a projection will expose a structural gap. The subtractive-power guarantee is proven only for one projection over a source; composition is untested, and evidence access in particular is expected not to compose.

payload.subject: Projection / participant-relative state
payload.statement: Participant-relative state requires no participant model: it reduces to observation access plus scope, with belief remaining an unresolved obligation.

payload.subject: Projection / invariant scope
payload.statement: Invariant scope needs one further distinction the current foundation lacks: an invariant that is unevaluable on a surface is not the same as one that is vacuously satisfied there.

payload.hypothesis: Termination is specific to search and planning substrates.
payload.reason: the FSM control reached termination first via accepting states, showing it is a general property of lawful evolution rather than a search idiosyncrasy. The contract had not merely omitted it — no prior substrate could exhibit it, because neither the field nor the governor ever finishes

payload.hypothesis: Projection composition preserves subtractive power: projecting a projection cannot launder what the first layer withheld.
A CompiledPattern is a complete world declaration, so the kernel can host field evolution directly.
payload.reason: lawful evolution exists only as executable force code, not as data. This falsification reframed the whole of Stage 1: the field became one execution substrate behind a contract rather than the world itself

payload.hypothesis: Field dynamics is fully expressible as declarative data given a sufficient expression IR.
payload.reason: 8 of 11 corpus laws were expressible; 3 remained opaque (hidden mutable state, host/callback dependence, nonlinear time). The IR was deliberately not expanded to force success, so the result stands as partial-with-opaque-extensions

payload.hypothesis: DynamicsExecutionContext.now is needed by real substrates.
payload.reason: four of four adapted substrates have now declined to use it — the field injects its own clock, the governor, FSM and planner have none. Evidence against is accumulating but the concept was deliberately kept rather than removed, since absence of use across four substrates is not proof of uselessness

payload.note: the substrate the contract was extracted from; establishes nothing about generality on its own

changes.rationale: declareTransitionLaw could be claimed truthfully with no way to obtain the law — a capability that cannot be exercised is incoherent
the capability and the accessor must agree in both directions

payload.role: control — expected to require zero change

changes.rationale: accepting states finish; without a generic terminal signal a kernel must read substrate-specific output to know whether to keep advancing

payload.note: PREDICTION FALSIFIED (predicted 0, actual 1). The control exposing the gap is the stronger result: termination is general, not search-specific.

payload.role: falsification candidate — terminates, partially declarable law

rejectedChanges.rationale: declareTransitionLaw:false already answers truthfully — this substrate cannot declare THE law; publishing part of it is a feature request, not a missing concept

payload.note: first substrate to exercise executionKind "hybrid", declared in F1.3 but never used until now
